text:
The injunctions as made at paragraph [para number] (above) shall continue until [date] unless previously varied or otherwise discharged by further order of the court.

The respondent, or each of them, shall:

By no later than [time] notify the solicitors for the applicant [applicant firm name], ([applicant firm address], [applicant firm DX], [applicant firm phone], [applicant firm email]) of the current whereabouts of the person to be protected [name] [and the current care arrangements for them];

By no later than [time] notify the solicitors for the applicant [applicant firm name], ([applicant firm address], [applicant firm DX], [applicant firm phone], [applicant firm email]) of the arrangements that will be made for the return of the person to be protected to the jurisdiction of England and Wales;

Return or cause the return of the person to be protected [name] to the jurisdiction of England and Wales in accordance with the arrangements notified at sub-paragraph b. (above) and by no later than [time and date].

Upon the return of the person to be protected [name] to the jurisdiction of England and Wales the respondent, or each of them, shall be prohibited from removing the said person to be protected from the jurisdiction of England and Wales, and from instructing or encouraging any other person to so remove them.

The matter shall be adjourned for [Final Hearing] / [further directions] / [review] before a [High Court Judge of the Family Division] / [Judge of the Family Court] sitting in chambers at [place] on [date] at [time], with a time estimate of [time estimate].

The respondent shall attend the hearing listed pursuant to paragraph [para number] (above) together with solicitors and/or counsel if so instructed. A penal notice is attached to this paragraph.

The respondent may, if so advised, file and serve a statement of evidence by no later than [time and date].

The [applicant] / [person to be protected] may, if so advised, file and serve a statement of evidence by no later than [time and date].

Costs reserved.

Dated [date]

Note to Arresting Officer:

Under section 63CA of the Family Law Act 1996, breach of a forced marriage protection order is a criminal offence punishable by up to five years’ imprisonment. It is an arrestable offence and it is not necessary to obtain a warrant.
